app.name: winapp CLI
app.one_liner: A unified command-line interface for Windows app development that abstracts packaging, identity, manifests, certificates, and SDK integration across diverse frameworks like Electron, .NET, CMake, and Rust.

screens.name: CLI Terminal Interface
screens.purpose: Provide a command-driven interface for executing Windows app development workflows including initialization, packaging, certificate management, manifest updates, and Store integration.
primary_actions: Initialize new projects with package identity
Package apps into MSIX format with signing
Update app manifests and assets (including SVG support)

rust_commands.name: certGenerate
rust_commands.purpose: Generate a self-signed PFX certificate for development and testing purposes.

rust_commands.name: certInfo
rust_commands.purpose: Display detailed information about a PFX certificate including subject, issuer, and validity period.

rust_commands.name: createExternalCatalog
rust_commands.purpose: Create an external catalog for asset management during packaging or deployment.

rust_commands.name: init
rust_commands.purpose: Initialize a project with package identity and basic configuration for Windows app development without full packaging.

rust_commands.name: manifestUpdateAssets
rust_commands.purpose: Update application assets in the manifest, supporting SVG input converted to required bitmap formats.

rust_commands.name: packageApp
rust_commands.purpose: Create an MSIX package from the project with optional signing and Store readiness checks.

rust_commands.name: store
rust_commands.purpose: Execute Microsoft Store developer CLI commands through an integrated subcommand.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add certGenerate and certInfo for local certificate lifecycle
week 1: Implement core init and packageApp commands with basic MSIX generation
week 1: Support manifest asset updates with PNG/JPG only (defer SVG)
week 2: Add createExternalCatalog for advanced packaging scenarios
week 2: Enable manifest placeholders and qualified names support
week 2: Integrate store subcommand for basic Store CLI passthrough
week 3: Add SVG-to-bitmap conversion in manifestUpdateAssets
week 3: Auto-generate typed npm exports for all commands
week 3: Validate .NET and Electron project compatibility
acceptance_tests: Given a .NET project, when I run 'winapp package-app', then a signed MSIX is produced and marked store-ready.
Given a dev.pfx certificate, when I run 'winapp cert info dev.pfx --password test', then subject and validity are displayed.
Given a new Electron project, when I run 'winapp init', then package identity is added without full packaging.
Given an app icon.svg, when I run 'winapp manifest update-assets --icon icon.svg', then required bitmap assets are generated.
